text: Wore these for my sister's sangeet and received compliments all evening. The sway is hypnotic — they feel substantial but never heavy. Packaging was beautiful too.
The medallion detail is exquisite. It sits perfectly at the collarbone and paired wonderfully with a deep maroon saree. You can tell these are made by hand.
Stunning enamel work. The clasp is clever — completely hidden. Only wish there was a larger size option, but the medium fits my wrist well.
This haar transformed my reception look. Light enough to wear for hours, yet every station catches light differently. Worth every rupee.
Pinned this on my mother's dupatta for Diwali — the zari embroidery is so fine you need to look closely. A small piece with enormous character.
Used three in my bridal braid. They looked like fresh jasmine from a distance! The jasmine scent option is subtle and lovely.
Beautiful craftsmanship. Took a week to arrive but the wait was justified. Slightly lighter than expected which is actually a plus for long events.
Gifted this to my wife who plays sitar — she was delighted by the detail. The silver has a warm tone that doesn't look mass-produced.
My go-to everyday earrings now. The posts don't irritate my sensitive ears. Simple but clearly well-made.
The adjustable chain made fitting easy for my bridal trial. The kundan teardrop is the perfect size — statement without overwhelming the face.
Elegant and understated. The silk thread between pearls gives it a softness that metal-only chokers lack. Would love a matching earring set.
Split the set with my two sisters for Eid — each bangle is unique enough to feel personal but they clearly belong together. Hammered texture is gorgeous.
